# Code of Conduct

This is a small project and I'd like to keep the bar simple: **be decent to each other.** Critique ideas, not people. Assume good faith until it's clearly unearned. When you disagree, say _why_ and cite the section.

## What I won't tolerate

- Harassment, slurs, or targeted unkindness toward any contributor.
- Doxxing or sharing private information without consent.
- Bad-faith argument — pretending not to understand, moving goalposts, refusing to engage with the strongest version of an opposing view.
- "Sea-lioning" or persistent demands for engagement in bad faith.
- Anything that would make a thoughtful person feel unwelcome to contribute.

## Enforcement

I'm the maintainer right now. If something goes wrong, email me at **[tamimbinhakim.work@gmail.com](mailto:tamimbinhakim.work@gmail.com)** with the link and a short description. I will:

1. Read what happened.
2. Talk to the people involved if useful.
3. Take whatever action I think is fair — which can range from "nothing, I think you're wrong" to "you're banned from the repo."

I'm not going to publish a formal escalation policy until the project is large enough to need one. If you think I'm handling something badly, you can tell me, and I'll listen.

## Scope

This applies in the issue tracker, in PRs, in any project-affiliated chat, and in spaces where someone is representing the project (talks, conference Q&A). It doesn't apply to your private life. I'm not policing what you do elsewhere unless it bleeds into how you treat people here.

— Tamim
